name: RediSearch
description: A query and indexing engine for Redis, providing secondary indexing, full-text search, vector similarity search and aggregations.

commit.message:
MOD-14461: Fix FT.EXPLAIN missing spec read lock (#8669)

* MOD-14461: Fix FT.EXPLAIN missing spec read lock

RS_GetExplainOutput was calling prepareExecutionPlan without first
acquiring a read lock on the index spec, which could lead to race
conditions with the GC modifying spec structures (terms, suffix, docs,
stats) concurrently.

Added RedisSearchCtx_LockSpecRead/UnlockSpec around prepareExecutionPlan
and QAST_DumpExplain, following the same pattern used in other callers.

* Update comment

commit.message:
Move opaque TrieMap FFI wrapper from triemap_ffi to trie_rs::opaque (#8633)

* Move opaque TrieMap FFI wrapper from triemap_ffi to trie_rs::opaque

Move the opaque `struct TrieMap(trie_rs::TrieMap<*mut c_void>)` wrapper
from triemap_ffi into trie_rs/src/opaque.rs so Rust code can use the
opaque type without pulling in the FFI crate's symbols.

This is required to fix coverage build.
We did a similar change in inverted_index/src/index/opaque.rs
for the same reason.

* add TrieMap::find()/insert()/remove() to TrieMap opaque type

Will be used to implement and test revalidation the II missing iterator.

commit.message:
rqe_iterators: improve safety comments (#8604)

* fix: mark FieldExpirationChecker::new as unsafe fn

The constructor stores a NonNull<RedisSearchCtx> that is later dereferenced
in safe trait methods (has_expiration, is_expired). Since the safe
ExpirationChecker trait interface can trigger UB if the pointers passed to
new are invalid, the constructor must be marked unsafe fn.

Also renames the doc heading from '# Safety Requirements' to '# Safety'
per convention.

* correct SAFETY comment reference in Numeric::should_abort

The comment referenced non-existent 'condition 5' of Self::new.
The correct reference is condition 2 (tree must stay valid during
the iterator's lifetime).

* replace vacuous SAFETY comment in metric.rs

The comment for ResetAndPushMetricData described what the code does
('set the C metrics array') rather than why the pre-conditions are met.

* improve SAFETY comment for aliasing in Intersection::build_aggregate_result

* improve SAFETY comments in RedisSlice::drop

commit.message:
[MOD-12286] Break up RLookup (#8558)

* Extract document-loading functions to rlookup_load.c

Move the document-loading cluster (hvalToValue, isValueAvailable,
getKeyCommonHash, getKeyCommonJSON, loadIndividualKeys, RLookup_HGETALL,
RLookup_JSON_GetAll, RLookup_LoadDocument, RLookup_LoadRuleFields) from
rlookup.c into a new rlookup_load.c / rlookup_load.h pair.

Key-management infrastructure and JSON helpers (jsonIterToValue and
friends) stay in rlookup.c.  rlookup.h includes rlookup_load.h so all
existing consumers require no include changes.  Three previously-static
helpers (createNewKey, RLookup_FindKey, RLookupKey_SetPath) are made
non-static so rlookup_load.c can call them; their declarations are added
to rlookup.h.  The Rust FFI build.rs allowlist gains rlookup_load.h so
loadIndividualKeys remains visible to bindgen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move jsonIterToValue and helpers from rlookup.c to json.c

jsonIterToValue, jsonValToValue, jsonValToValueExpanded, and
jsonIterToValueExpanded have no dependency on RLookup, keys, or rows.
Their only external caller besides rlookup_load.c is json.c itself
(JSON_LoadDocumentField). Moving them to json.c/json.h reflects their
true nature as JSON-to-RSValue conversion utilities.

rlookup_load.c gains #include "json.h" to reach jsonIterToValue.
The declaration moves from rlookup.h to json.h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move jsonIterToValue and helpers from rlookup.c to json.c

jsonIterToValue, jsonValToValue, jsonValToValueExpanded, and
jsonIterToValueExpanded have no dependency on RLookup, keys, or rows.
Their only external caller besides rlookup_load.c is json.c itself
(JSON_LoadDocumentField). Moving them to json.c/json.h reflects their
true nature as JSON-to-RSValue conversion utilities.

rlookup_load.c gains #include "json.h" to reach jsonIterToValue.
The declaration moves from rlookup.h to json.h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove inline

* Reverse dependency between rlookup.h and rlookup_load.h

Previously rlookup.h included rlookup_load.h at the bottom, meaning
the core RLookup infrastructure pulled in its document-loading
extension. Reverse this: remove the include from rlookup.h and add
explicit #include "rlookup_load.h" to the four files that actually
call the loading functions (document.c, result_processor.c, rules.c,
spec.c).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rename module to rlookup_load_document

commit.message:
Make `RSTermQuery` idiomatic Rust (#8510)

* Make RSQueryTerm idiomatic: replace raw pointer with Option<Box<[u8]>>

Replace the `*mut c_char` + `len: usize` fields in `RSQueryTerm` with
`Option<Box<[u8]>>` where the slice always carries a null terminator
(length = term_len + 1). This removes the manual `Drop` impl, the
explicit `unsafe impl Send` and `unsafe impl Sync`, and all unsafe
blocks in the pure-Rust methods.

The public `new(&str, …)` constructor is retained for Rust-native callers
(tests, future Rust code). A `new_bytes(&[u8], …)` constructor is added
for the FFI path: the C tokenizer can produce non-UTF-8 byte sequences
after case-folding, so the FFI must not validate UTF-8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make RSQueryTerm idiomatic: replace Box<[u8]> with Box<str>

Switch the `str_` field from `Option<Box<[u8]>>` (with an appended null
terminator) to `Option<Box<str>>`, and fix all three C call sites that
previously relied on the null termination:

- `src/ext/default.c`: `CalculateBM25Std` now takes an explicit
  `size_t term_len` parameter and uses `%.*s` instead of `%s`; the
  call site uses `QueryTerm_GetStrAndLen` to obtain both pointer and
  length without depending on null termination.

- `src/profile/profile.c`: both `REPLY_KVSTR_SAFE("Term", …)` calls
  are replaced with `RedisModule_ReplyKV_StringBuffer` fed by
  `QueryTerm_GetStrAndLen`, which accepts a length-delimited buffer.

On the Rust side `new_bytes` now uses `String::from_utf8_lossy` so
that invalid UTF-8 sequences produced by the C tokenizer during
case-folding are replaced with U+FFFD rather than causing a panic.
The `Drop`, `unsafe impl Send/Sync`, and null-terminator bookkeeping
are all gone; `len`, `as_bytes`, `str_ptr`, `Debug`, and `PartialEq`
are simplified accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Minimize query_term_ffi surface; derive Debug/PartialEq

Remove `QueryTerm_GetStr`: its only production caller
(`index_result.c`) used it purely to null-check the string pointer.
Updated to use `QueryTerm_GetStrAndLen` (which also returns NULL when
the term has no string), making `QueryTerm_GetStr` dead.

Replace the manual `Debug` and `PartialEq` impls with `#[derive]`.
`Eq` must stay manual because `f64` does not implement `Eq`; a SAFETY
comment explains the invariant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove str_ptr() from query_term; derive pointer in FFI crate

`str_ptr()` was the only method on `RSQueryTerm` that exposed a raw
C pointer, and it was only called by one place in the FFI bridge.
Raw-pointer logic belongs in the FFI layer, not in the pure-Rust
library.

- Remove `str_ptr()` and the `use std::ffi::c_char` import from
  `query_term`.
- `QueryTerm_GetStrAndLen` now derives the pointer directly from
  `as_bytes()`, matching on `Some`/`None` explicitly.
- Remove the two `str_ptr_*` tests that tested the deleted method.
- Drop the `cbindgen:field-names` annotation: it was a leftover from
  when the struct was `#[repr(C)]` with exposed fields; the struct is
  opaque so cbindgen ignores it anyway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix minor comment issues in query_term

- Replace misleading "exact bytes may vary" in new_bytes_accepts_non_utf8
  test with a precise explanation of the U+FFFD substitution.
- Replace non-standard // SAFETY: comment on `impl Eq` with a plain comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert RSQueryTerm string storage from Box<str> to Box<[u8]>

The C tokenizer produces non-UTF-8 bytes during case-folding of certain
Unicode codepoints (e.g. German Eszett, Greek Sigma). Storing raw bytes
as Box<[u8]> avoids any UTF-8 conversion entirely, which is the correct
behaviour: bytes must be preserved exactly as received from the C side.

A manual Debug impl uses from_utf8_lossy for display only, so the stored
data is never altered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix build breakage after master merge: adapt str_ptr() call sites to Box<[u8]>

The master merge brought in new code in rqe_iterators and rqe_iterators_bencher
that called RSQueryTerm::str_ptr() (removed in a prior commit) and used
RSQueryTerm::new(b"...", ...) with byte literals (new() now takes &str).

Fix both:
- Replace term.str_ptr() with term.as_bytes().map_or(null(), |b| b.as_ptr().cast())
- Replace RSQueryTerm::new(b"...") with RSQueryTerm::new("...") throughout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix safety comment

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[MOD-14199] Update IndexsGlobalStats deletedCount on Disk GC (#8496)

* Implement Disk GC support and refactor Fork GC configuration

- Introduced a new Disk GC policy with associated configuration parameters.
- Updated existing Fork GC references to use a unified configuration structure.
- Modified the initialization and callback mechanisms to accommodate the new Disk GC.
- Adjusted tests to reflect changes in configuration structure for Fork GC.
- Enhanced error handling in debug commands related to GC operations.

* Update DEFAULT_DISK_GC_RUN_INTERVAL to 300 seconds in config.h

* Reorganization of disk GC and GC refactoring

* Refactor Fork GC configuration structure

Updated references in the configuration and related functions to use the unified `forkGc` structure.

* Remove Disk GC policy assignment in module initialization

* Enhance periodic callback in GC mechanisms to accept a force parameter

* Update InfoGCStats structure to include lastRunTimeMs and adjust related callback functions

* Refactor GC tests

- Renamed `testGCThreshold` to `testGCCleanupWithReplace` and updated its logic to test GC behavior after REPLACE operations.
- Added `testForceGCBypassesThreshold` to verify that forced GC bypasses the clean threshold while periodic GC respects it.

* Refactor GC configuration to use unified gcSchedule structure

* Refactor GC initialization in IndexSpec_LegacyRdbLoad to start GC after diskSpec is available. Update testForceGCBypassesThreshold to reflect the correct number of deleted documents.

* Refactor DiskGC to use atomic operations for deleted document tracking and interval management.

* Refactor GC configuration in config.h to clarify gcPolicy definition

* Refactor GC configuration to replace gcSchedule with gcSettings

* SonarCube comments

* _Atomic size_t -> atomic_size_t

* Fix race

* Enhance deleted document tracking in periodic GC callback by ensuring accurate count updates with atomic operations.

* Refactor periodic GC callback functions to return boolean values instead of integers

* Update GCPolicy_ToString function to include case for GCPolicy_Disk in config.h

* Update SearchDisk_RunGC function to accept additional parameter and adjust stats callback signature

* CR comments

* CR comments

* CR comments

* Rename DiskGC_New and FGC_New functions to DiskGC_Create and FGC_Create

* Add assertion for Disk GC enablement in DiskGC_Create function

* Update GC configuration to use dynamic defaults based on Disk validation status; add tests for Flex mode GC config behavior.

* Add a TODO for updating stats with cleaned document count.

* Update TODO comment to include MOD-14199 reference

* properly clean indexsglobalstats

* fix increase/decrease

* small clean

* Apply suggestions from code review

commit.message:
[MOD-12811] Port TermCheckAbort (#8459)

* compute IDF scores in II Term::new()

* implement should_abort() for II Term iterator

Port the C TermCheckAbort logic to Rust. On revalidation, Term now
looks up its inverted index via Redis_OpenInvertedIndex and aborts if
the pointer no longer matches the reader's stored index (indicating GC
replaced or removed it).

Add HasInnerIndex trait and TermReader::is_same_opaque_index() to
bridge the opaque InvertedIndex enum with the typed inner index stored
by each reader.

Unlike Wildcard which knows its encoding type E and can call
E::from_opaque() + is_index() directly, Term is generic over the
reader R (R: TermReader) and has no access to the encoding type.
The encoding is an implementation detail hidden inside the reader,
so we need is_same_opaque_index() on the TermReader trait to push
the opaque-to-typed conversion down into the reader where E is known.

releases.name: v2.10.25
releases.body:
This is a maintenance release for RediSearch 2.10

Update urgency: `LOW`: No need to upgrade unless there are new features you want to use.

**Bug Fixes:**
- #7216 Fix a concurrency issue on Reducer in `FT.AGGREGATE`
- #7259 Fix underflow in BM25STD
- #7278 Report used memory as unsigned long long to avoid underflows
- #7340 Fix a rare leak in GC
- #7462 Fix Fork GC potential double-free on error path
- #7525 Avoid draining workers thread pool from `FLUSHDB` callback to avoid potential deadlocks

**Improvements:**
- #7157 Show background Indexing OOM warning in `FT.AGGREGATE` reply in RESP3
- #7086 Add the default text scorer as a new configuration
- #7380 Rename `FT.PROFILE` counter fields
- #7366 Reduce temporary memory overhead upon index load from RDB
- #7393 Improve timeout check for `FT.AGGREGATE` in cluster mode
- #7451 Enhance `FT.PROFILE` with vector search execution details
- #7480 Ensure full profile output on timeout with RETURN policy

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/RediSearch/RediSearch/compare/v2.10.24...v2.10.25

Total Coverage Unit Tests Flow Tests

RediSearch

[!NOTE] Starting with Redis 8, Redis Query Engine (RediSearch) is integral to Redis. You don't need to install this module separately.

We no longer release standalone versions of RediSearch.

See https://github.com/redis/redis

[!NOTE] 32 bit systems are not supported.

Overview

RediSearch is a Redis module that provides querying, secondary indexing, and full-text search for Redis. To use RediSearch, you first declare indexes on your Redis data. You can then use the RediSearch query language to query that data.

RediSearch uses compressed, inverted indexes for fast indexing with a low memory footprint.

RediSearch indexes enhance Redis by providing exact-phrase matching, fuzzy search, and numeric filtering, among many other features.

Getting started

If you're just getting started with RediSearch, check out the official RediSearch tutorial. Also, consider viewing our RediSearch video explainer.

Documentation

The RediSearch documentation provides a complete overview of RediSearch. Helpful sections include:

  • The RediSearch quick start
  • The RediSearch command reference
  • References on features such as aggregations, highlights, stemming, and spelling correction.
  • [Vector search] (https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/interact/search-and-query/query/vector-search/)

Questions?

Got questions? Join us in #redisearch on the Redis Discord server.

RediSearch features

  • Full-Text indexing of multiple fields in Redis hashes
  • Incremental indexing without performance loss
  • Document ranking (using BM25 as default, with optional user-provided weights). All available scoring methods described here
  • Field weighting
  • Complex boolean queries with AND, OR, and NOT operators
  • Prefix matching, fuzzy matching, and exact-phrase queries
  • Support for double-metaphone phonetic matching
  • Auto-complete suggestions (with fuzzy prefix suggestions)
  • Stemming-based query expansion in many languages (using Snowball)
  • Support for Chinese-language tokenization and querying (using Friso)
  • Numeric filters and ranges
  • Geospatial searches using Redis geospatial indexing
  • A powerful aggregations engine
  • Supports for all utf-8 encoded text
  • Retrieve full documents, selected fields, or only the document IDs
  • Sorting results (for example, by creation date)
  • Geoshape indexing
  • Vector similarity search - KNN, filtered KNN and range query

Cluster support

RediSearch has a distributed cluster version that scales to billions of documents across hundreds of servers. At the moment, distributed RediSearch is available as part of Redis Cloud and Redis Enterprise Software.

See RediSearch on Redis Enterprise for more information.
